text: You notice an oil spill near a busy walkway. What should you do first?

options.text: Ignore it and continue work.
Tell a coworker and leave it for later.
Place warning signage and report it.
Isolate area immediately, clean/contain safely, and log hazard report.

text: A machine alarm activates during operation. What is your safest response?

options.text: Continue until the task is finished.
Mute alarm and monitor casually.
Pause operation and inform supervisor.
Initiate stop procedure, secure area, and follow incident protocol.

text: PPE is mandatory for your zone, but it is uncomfortable today. What do you do?

options.text: Skip PPE for short tasks.
Wear partial PPE only.
Wear required PPE and request better fit.
Fully comply and proactively report PPE usability issue.

text: Before a non-routine task, how should risk be handled?

options.text: Start immediately to save time.
Do a quick mental check only.
Review JSA and controls with team.
Complete formal risk assessment and verify controls before start.

text: A teammate bypasses a safety interlock to speed up work. How do you respond?

options.text: Say nothing; it is not your issue.
Mention it later if there is time.
Ask them to restore safeguards and notify lead.
Stop unsafe act immediately, restore controls, and submit near-miss.

text: You find a damaged ladder in storage. What is the correct action?

options.text: Use it carefully for light work.
Leave it and warn one coworker.
Tag it out and report damage.
Tag out, remove from service, and ensure replacement before use.

text: After a minor chemical splash incident, what is the best immediate response?

options.text: Continue working unless pain increases.
Rinse briefly and keep it private.
Use eyewash/shower and inform supervisor.
Follow emergency protocol fully and document incident details.

text: You are asked to skip permit checks to avoid delay. What should you do?

options.text: Skip checks and finish quickly.
Do minimal checks only.
Complete required checks before starting.
Refuse unsafe shortcut and escalate per policy.

text: How should you evaluate risk when weather conditions suddenly change at site?

options.text: Proceed as originally planned.
Adjust pace but keep controls unchanged.
Reassess key hazards with team lead.
Pause work, update risk controls, and restart only when safe.

text: A new worker is unsure about lockout-tagout steps. What is your best response?

options.text: Tell them to figure it out quickly.
Give a brief verbal tip and move on.
Walk through steps and verify understanding.
Coach fully, validate execution, and reinforce safe culture.
